tossups.question: In a Madame d’Aulnoy fairytale, to escape an ogre who is using these objects, a shipwrecked princess transforms herself into a bee and a prince into an orange tree. After an ogre falls asleep, the tiny Hop-o’-My-Thumb steals these objects from him in a Charles Perrault fairytale. A figure from another story receives these objects from a miller whom he props up as the Marquis of Carabas, and wins a castle by tricking a shapeshifting ogre into turning into a (*) mouse. A group of beings who make these objects quit and never return after they receive a gift of beautiful clothes. After improperly bringing these objects to church, Karen seeks help from an executioner to escape them in a Hans Christian Andersen story. One variant of these objects is named for “seven-leagues.” These red-hot objects are used to punish the Evil Queen in “Snow White.” For 10 points, name these objects, worn by a folkloric cat.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a Madame d'Aulnoy fairytale, to escape an ogre who is using these objects, a shipwrecked princess transforms herself into a bee and a prince into an orange tree. After an ogre falls asleep, the tiny Hop-o'-My-Thumb steals these objects from him in a Charles Perrault fairytale. A figure from another story receives these objects from a miller whom he props up as the Marquis of Carabas, and wins a castle by tricking a shapeshifting ogre into turning into a (*) mouse. A group of beings who make these objects quit and never return after they receive a gift of beautiful clothes. After improperly bringing these objects to church, Karen seeks help from an executioner to escape them in a Hans Christian Andersen story. One variant of these objects is named for "seven-leagues." These red-hot objects are used to punish the Evil Queen in "Snow White." For 10 points, name these objects, worn by a folkloric cat.
tossups.answer: shoes [accept seven-league boots; or bottes de sept lieues; or Puss in Boots; accept glass slippers] (The post power-break clue refers to the Shoemaker and the Elves.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: shoes [accept seven-league boots; or bottes de sept lieues; or Puss in Boots; accept glass slippers] (The post power-break clue refers to the Shoemaker and the Elves.)

tossups.question: One of these animals helped a prince complete a king's tasks using a dog in an acorn and cloth in a walnut before helping with a final task by having him decapitate her. Another one of these animals slays a shapeshifting ogre in the process of helping Constantino acquire a castle to trick a king. Sir Kay battled one of these animals of Palug. One of these animals from the stories of Reynard the Fox named Tibert claims the title of king of these animals, a title also held by a creature named [this animal]-síth (“shee”). One of these animals named (*) Grimalkin stalks the Scottish highlands, and appears in one of the first English novels. Freya’s chariot is driven using two of these animals. For 10 points, name these animals, whose black versions are associated with halloween and bad luck.
tossups.question_sanitized: One of these animals helped a prince complete a king's tasks using a dog in an acorn and cloth in a walnut before helping with a final task by having him decapitate her. Another one of these animals slays a shapeshifting ogre in the process of helping Constantino acquire a castle to trick a king. Sir Kay battled one of these animals of Palug. One of these animals from the stories of Reynard the Fox named Tibert claims the title of king of these animals, a title also held by a creature named [this animal]-sith ("shee"). One of these animals named (*) Grimalkin stalks the Scottish highlands, and appears in one of the first English novels. Freya's chariot is driven using two of these animals. For 10 points, name these animals, whose black versions are associated with halloween and bad luck.
tossups.answer: cats [accept black cats; accept white cats; accept cath] (Clues include Madame d’Aulnoy’s The White Cat, Puss in Boots, and William Baldwin’s Beware the Cat.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: cats [accept black cats; accept white cats; accept cath] (Clues include Madame d'Aulnoy's The White Cat, Puss in Boots, and William Baldwin's Beware the Cat.)

bonuses.leadin: A white one of these animals who runs a marijuana greenhouse in Colorado partially titles a Kelly Link collection of fairy tale retellings. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A white one of these animals who runs a marijuana greenhouse in Colorado partially titles a Kelly Link collection of fairy tale retellings. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these animals, one of which is a transformed princess who aids one brother in a set of impossible tasks. An anthropomorphic one of these animals appears “in boots.”

A corpse must be left out to avoid the horrors of the “White Road” in Link’s retelling of a tale about four animals from Bremen who have this occupation. A figure with this occupation serves as a ratcatcher in another German town.

The lesbian narrator feels the moon influencing her in a Link retelling inspired by a tale about a boy who “does not know” this emotion. In the Dionysiaca, two Greek gods of this emotion serve as Dionysus’s charioteers.
parts_sanitized: Name these animals, one of which is a transformed princess who aids one brother in a set of impossible tasks. An anthropomorphic one of these animals appears "in boots."

A corpse must be left out to avoid the horrors of the "White Road" in Link's retelling of a tale about four animals from Bremen who have this occupation. A figure with this occupation serves as a ratcatcher in another German town.

The lesbian narrator feels the moon influencing her in a Link retelling inspired by a tale about a boy who "does not know" this emotion. In the Dionysiaca, two Greek gods of this emotion serve as Dionysus's charioteers.
answers: cats [accept “The White Cat” or White Cat, Black Dog; accept Puss in Boots]
musicians [accept “The Town Musicians of Bremen” or “The Bremen Town Musicians” or “Die Bremer Stadtmusikanten”; accept Pied Piper of Hamelin or Pan Piper of Hamelin]

fear [or being afraid or scared or clear equivalents; accept Deimos or Phobos; accept “The Boy Who Did Not Know Fear” or “The Girl Who Did Not Know Fear” or “The Story of a Boy Who Went Forth to Learn Fear” or “The Story of the Youth Who Went Forth to Learn What Fear Was” or “Märchen von einem, der auszog das Fürchten zu lernen”]
answers_sanitized: cats [accept "The White Cat" or White Cat, Black Dog; accept Puss in Boots]
musicians [accept "The Town Musicians of Bremen" or "The Bremen Town Musicians" or "Die Bremer Stadtmusikanten"; accept Pied Piper of Hamelin or Pan Piper of Hamelin]

fear [or being afraid or scared or clear equivalents; accept Deimos or Phobos; accept "The Boy Who Did Not Know Fear" or "The Girl Who Did Not Know Fear" or "The Story of a Boy Who Went Forth to Learn Fear" or "The Story of the Youth Who Went Forth to Learn What Fear Was" or "Marchen von einem, der auszog das Furchten zu lernen"]

packet.name: D - Chicago B, McGill A, UBC A, UNC A

bonuses.leadin: A genderbent version of this character storms out after learning that her ungrateful master planned to throw her dead body out a window in a Giambattista Basile tale. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this compassionate fae who feigns the drowning of “Lucky Constantino” so that he can marry the princess. In another tale, this character dresses up a poor miller’s son as the Marquis of Carabas.

In Perrault’s (“peh-ROH’s”) account, Puss consumes an ogre in the guise of this animal to secure a castle for the Marquis. Six of these animals transform into dapple gray horses in Perrault’s version of “Cinderella.”

In another Perrault tale, a character named “Hop on My [this object]” tricks an ogre into killing his daughters by exchanging their crowns with nightcaps. A Round Table knight named for this object dies from a spider-bite.
answers: Puss in Boots [or Der gestiefelte Kater, Il gatto con gli stival, le Maître chat, or Le Chat botté; accept Master Cat; prompt on Cat as a Helper] (“Lucky Constantino” is by Giovanni Straparola. The Marquis of Carabas is from Perrault’s account.)
thumb [accept Hop on My Thumb, Hop o’ My Thumb, Little Thumbling, Little Thumb, Little Poucet, Le petit Poucet; accept Tom Thumb]
answers_sanitized: Puss in Boots [or Der gestiefelte Kater, Il gatto con gli stival, le Maitre chat, or Le Chat botte; accept Master Cat; prompt on Cat as a Helper] ("Lucky Constantino" is by Giovanni Straparola. The Marquis of Carabas is from Perrault's account.)
thumb [accept Hop on My Thumb, Hop o' My Thumb, Little Thumbling, Little Thumb, Little Poucet, Le petit Poucet; accept Tom Thumb]

parts_sanitized: Name this compassionate fae who feigns the drowning of "Lucky Constantino" so that he can marry the princess. In another tale, this character dresses up a poor miller's son as the Marquis of Carabas.

In another Perrault tale, a character named "Hop on My [this object]" tricks an ogre into killing his daughters by exchanging their crowns with nightcaps. A Round Table knight named for this object dies from a spider-bite.

bonuses.leadin: This author coined the difficult-to-translate term “waldeinsamkeit” in his supernatural short story “Der Blonde Eckbert.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this German Romantic, whose travels with his close friend Wilhelm Wackenroder led to his novel Franz Sternbald’s Wanderings. His novella about the “fair Magelone” inspired a Brahms song cycle.

In a play by Tieck, a fictional audience reacts to a performance of this fairy tale. In the version recorded by Charles Perrault, the title animal uses trickeration to help a poor miller’s son marry a princess.

In 1875, this author translated many of Perrault’s fairy tales, including Puss in Boots, into Italian. He may be better known for his novel about Gepetto, a woodcarver who creates a living puppet called Pinocchio.
answers: Puss in Boots [or Master Cat; or Der gestiefelte Kater]
answers_sanitized: Puss in Boots [or Master Cat; or Der gestiefelte Kater]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This author coined the difficult-to-translate term "waldeinsamkeit" in his supernatural short story "Der Blonde Eckbert." For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this German Romantic, whose travels with his close friend Wilhelm Wackenroder led to his novel Franz Sternbald's Wanderings. His novella about the "fair Magelone" inspired a Brahms song cycle.

In 1875, this author translated many of Perrault's fairy tales, including Puss in Boots, into Italian. He may be better known for his novel about Gepetto, a woodcarver who creates a living puppet called Pinocchio.

bonuses.leadin: One character in this story has the ability to change into a lion and an elephant. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Charles Perrault story about a character who works for the Marquis of Carabas by obtaining rare treasures for him. The title character stops chasing mice at the end of this story after encountering an ogre.

Le Maistre Chat is a French story in this genre. Other stories in this genre include one about a girl nicknamed “Aschenputtel” in Cendrillion, and the Brothers Grimm were popular authors of this genre.
The title character of this bizarre, morose fairy tale by Hans Christian Andersen prevails over Babette by kissing Rudy, causing him to drown the night before his wedding.
answers_sanitized: Le Maistre Chat [or Puss in Boots]
"The Ice-Maiden" [or "Iisjomfruen"; accept reasonable translations like "The Ice Girl"]
